enrichment.short_description: Mend.io is an application security platform offering SCA, SAST, and AI risk governance for enterprise development teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mend.io: enterprise application security combining SCA, SAST, dependency automation, and AI model/agent risk governance.

features.title: Software Composition Analysis (SCA)
features.description: Scans open source dependencies for known vulnerabilities across the codebase.

features.title: High-accuracy SAST
features.description: Static application security testing for proprietary code, including AI-generated code security checks.

features.title: AI-BOM and Shadow AI discovery
features.description: Surfaces every component in a codebase, including packages, AI models, agents, and system prompts.

features.title: Automated AI red teaming
features.description: Tests AI models and agents for security risks through automated adversarial testing.

features.title: System prompt hardening
features.description: Applies in-app runtime guardrails to reduce prompt injection and related AI risks.

features.title: Mend Renovate (automated dependency updates)
features.description: Automates dependency updates at scale with Merge Confidence ratings and workflows.

features.title: DAST and API security (add-ons)
features.description: Optional dynamic application security testing and API security testing available as add-ons.

pros: Combines traditional application security (SCA/SAST) with AI model and agent security testing in one platform
Automated dependency updates (Mend Renovate) reduce manual patching effort
Used by large enterprises including Microsoft, Google, Vodafone, Yahoo, and Siemens
Publishes transparent per-developer pricing with no additional per-GB fees
cons: Full AppSec suite pricing runs up to $1000 per developer per year, which can be costly for large teams
DAST, API Security, and EOL support are separate add-ons billed at custom pricing rather than included by default
No free plan or free trial is listed; access requires scheduling a demo

faqs.question: What does Mend.io do?
faqs.answer: Mend.io is an application security and AI security platform that scans code and dependencies for vulnerabilities and tests AI models and agents for security risks.

faqs.question: Does Mend.io offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: The Mend.io website does not list a free trial or free plan; it offers a 'Schedule a Demo' option instead.

faqs.question: How is Mend.io priced?
faqs.answer: Mend.io publishes per-developer annual pricing: up to $1000/dev/year for Mend AppSec, up to $300/dev/year for Mend AI, and up to $250/dev/year for Mend Renovate Enterprise.

faqs.question: What is Mend AI?
faqs.answer: Mend AI is Mend.io's product for AI-BOM and Shadow AI discovery, system prompt hardening, automated red teaming, and in-app runtime guardrails.

faqs.question: Who uses Mend.io?
faqs.answer: Mend.io lists enterprise customers including Microsoft, Google, Vodafone, Yahoo, and Siemens.

use_cases.title: Open source dependency security
use_cases.description: Scan and automatically update open source dependencies using SCA and Mend Renovate.

use_cases.title: AI application governance
use_cases.description: Discover AI models, agents, and system prompts in use, then test and harden them against security risks.
use_cases.audience: Teams building or deploying AI-powered applications

use_cases.title: Static code security testing
use_cases.description: Run SAST scans on proprietary code, including checks tailored to AI-generated code.

pricing_plans.price: Up to $1000 per developer per year
Up to $300 per developer per year
Up to $250 per developer per year

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mend.io?
paragraphs: Mend.io is an application security and AI security platform that scans proprietary code and open source dependencies for vulnerabilities while also testing AI models and agents for security risks.

The platform includes SCA, SAST, DAST, API security testing, and automated dependency management through Mend Renovate, alongside AI-BOM discovery, system prompt hardening, and automated AI red teaming.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mend.io is for
paragraphs: Mend.io is built for enterprise application security and development teams managing complex software supply chains, including organizations that build or deploy AI models and agents. Listed customers include Microsoft, Google, Vodafone, Yahoo, and Siemens.

enrichment.short_description: SonarQube is an automated code review platform for code quality, security, and AI-generated code assurance across 40+ languages.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SonarQube: automated code review for quality, security, and AI CodeFix across 40+ languages, with free and paid plans.

features.title: Automated code review
features.description: Integrates with development workflows to review code automatically on every change.

features.title: Security vulnerability detection
features.description: Detects complex vulnerabilities and security hotspots in code.

features.title: Quality metrics tracking
features.description: Tracks maintainability, reliability, and technical debt over time.

features.title: AI CodeFix
features.description: Provides context-aware, one-click fix suggestions for bugs and security issues using LLMs.

features.title: AI Code Assurance
features.description: Validates AI-generated code for security and quality before merging.

features.title: Secrets detection
features.description: Catches exposed code secrets during development.

features.title: Compliance reporting
features.description: Automates proof of code compliance for standards such as OWASP, CWE, and PCI DSS (Enterprise).

features.title: 40+ language support
features.description: Covers languages including Java, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C#, C++, PHP, Go, Rust, and Kotlin, plus IaC formats like Terraform and Kubernetes.

pros: Free tier and free IDE extension (SonarQube for IDE) available for individual use
Used by over 7 million developers and organizations including Mercedes-Benz, Adobe, Nvidia, eBay, Santander, and NASA
Supports 40+ programming languages and infrastructure-as-code formats
Native integrations with GitHub, Bitbucket, GitLab, Azure DevOps, Jira, and Slack
Named a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant
cons: Free tier is limited to private projects up to 50,000 lines of code
Enterprise features (SSO, SCIM, unlimited users/projects, advanced compliance) require custom Enterprise pricing
Advanced Security add-on (CVE detection, malicious package detection, SBOM) is billed separately at custom pricing

faqs.question: Does SonarQube have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, SonarQube offers a free tier for private projects up to 50,000 lines of code, plus a free SonarQube for IDE extension.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, SonarQube offers a 14-day free trial for the Team plan.

faqs.question: How much does the Team plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Team plan starts at $34 per month and is recommended for teams of fewer than 50 developers.

faqs.question: What does the Enterprise plan add?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan adds advanced security reports, audit logs, SSO/SCIM, unlimited users and projects, enterprise hierarchy and portfolios, and premium support, at custom pricing.

faqs.question: How many languages does SonarQube support?
faqs.answer: SonarQube supports 40+ programming languages, with the Enterprise plan adding languages such as ABAP, COBOL, and Apex.

faqs.question: Does SonarQube help with AI-generated code?
faqs.answer: Yes, through AI Code Assurance, which validates AI-generated code for security and quality, and AI CodeFix, which suggests one-click remediations.

use_cases.title: Automated code quality review
use_cases.description: Continuously review code for maintainability, reliability, and technical debt as part of CI/CD.

use_cases.title: Security vulnerability and secrets detection
use_cases.description: Detect vulnerabilities, security hotspots, and exposed secrets before code reaches production.

use_cases.title: AI-generated code assurance
use_cases.description: Validate and remediate AI-generated code using AI Code Assurance and AI CodeFix.

use_cases.title: Compliance reporting at scale
use_cases.description: Automate proof of code compliance against standards like OWASP, CWE, and PCI DSS.

features: Private project up to 50k lines of code
Advanced security reports and audit logs
OWASP, CWE, PCI DSS, MISRA C++:2023 compliance

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SonarQube?
paragraphs: SonarQube is an automated code review platform, available as SonarQube Cloud, SonarQube Server (self-managed), and a free SonarQube for IDE extension, that analyzes code for quality, reliability, and security issues.
It supports 40+ programming languages and infrastructure-as-code formats, offers AI Code Assurance to validate AI-generated code, and AI CodeFix to generate one-click remediation suggestions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SonarQube is for
paragraphs: SonarQube serves individual developers through its free tier and IDE extension, as well as teams and enterprises needing compliance reporting, advanced security scanning, and SSO/SCIM support. It is used by over 7 million developers and organizations including Mercedes-Benz, Adobe, Nvidia, eBay, Santander, and NASA.

enrichment.short_description: Checkmarx One is an AI-powered application security platform offering SAST, DAST, SCA, and cloud-native security scanning for enterprise development teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Checkmarx One: AI-powered application security testing with SAST, DAST, SCA, secrets, IaC, and API security scanning for enterprises.

features.title: NG SAST
features.description: Static application security testing that analyzes source code to find vulnerabilities during development.

features.title: DAST
features.description: Dynamic application security testing that assesses running applications for exploitable vulnerabilities.

features.title: SCA (Software Composition Analysis)
features.description: Scans open-source dependencies for known vulnerabilities and license risks.

features.title: Container Security
features.description: Scans container images and configurations for security issues before deployment.

features.title: Secrets Detection
features.description: Identifies hardcoded secrets and credentials in source code repositories.

features.title: IaC Security
features.description: Scans infrastructure-as-code templates for misconfigurations.

features.title: API Security
features.description: Discovers and tests APIs for vulnerabilities across the software supply chain.

features.title: AI-powered agents
features.description: Includes Developer Assist and Triage & Remediation Assist agents, plus a Checkmarx MCP Server, to help developers fix findings faster.

pros: Broad scanning coverage spanning SAST, DAST, SCA, container, secrets, IaC, and API security in one platform
AI-powered remediation and triage agents aim to reduce manual fix time
Recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Software Supply Chain Security
Holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 certification, and FedRAMP authorization
Reports over 1,800 enterprise customers and 40%+ of the Fortune 500 as users
cons: Pricing is fully custom and not published; requires a sales conversation to get a quote
No free trial is advertised on the site
Modular add-on structure (engines, AI agents, governance) can make total cost hard to estimate upfront

faqs.question: How is Checkmarx One priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is quote-based, calculated on developers, apps, and usage; a sales representative builds a custom proposal, typically provided within one business day.

faqs.question: Does Checkmarx offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: The website does not advertise a free trial; pricing and access are handled through a custom sales quote.

faqs.question: What scanning capabilities does Checkmarx One include?
faqs.answer: The platform includes NG SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, secrets detection, IaC security, API security, repository health, and malicious package protection, offered as modular components.

faqs.question: Does Checkmarx use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Checkmarx One includes AI-powered agents such as Developer Assist and Triage & Remediation Assist, plus a Checkmarx MCP Server.

faqs.question: Is Checkmarx suitable for large enterprises?
faqs.answer: Checkmarx states it is used by over 1,800 enterprises and by more than 40% of the Fortune 500.

use_cases.title: Secure the software development lifecycle
use_cases.description: Teams scan source code, dependencies, containers, and infrastructure-as-code for vulnerabilities throughout development.

use_cases.title: Reduce remediation time with AI agents
use_cases.description: Developer Assist and Triage & Remediation Assist agents help developers triage and fix flagged vulnerabilities faster.

use_cases.title: Manage application security posture at scale
use_cases.description: ASPM and AI-BOM capabilities give governance teams visibility into risk across many applications and AI components.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Checkmarx?
paragraphs: Checkmarx One is an AI-powered application security testing platform that combines static analysis (SAST), dynamic analysis (DAST), software composition analysis (SCA), container security, secrets detection, infrastructure-as-code security, and API security in a single platform.

The platform adds AI-powered agents, including Developer Assist and Triage & Remediation Assist, along with a Checkmarx MCP Server, to help teams triage and remediate findings. Checkmarx reports scanning over 800 billion lines of code monthly and serving over 1,800 enterprise customers, including more than 40% of the Fortune 500.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Checkmarx is for
paragraphs: Checkmarx is built for enterprise application security and DevSecOps teams that need broad, unified vulnerability scanning across code, dependencies, containers, and cloud infrastructure, along with governance features like ASPM and AI-BOM for tracking AI usage in software.

enrichment.short_description: Veracode is an application security platform offering SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, and AI-assisted flaw remediation for enterprises.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Veracode application security platform: SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, and AI-powered remediation for enterprise software teams.

features.title: SAST
features.description: Finds and helps fix security flaws as code is written.

features.title: DAST
features.description: Finds and helps fix runtime vulnerabilities in running web applications.

features.title: SCA (Software Composition Analysis)
features.description: Identifies vulnerabilities in open-source code dependencies.

features.title: Container Security
features.description: Secures container technologies before they reach production.

features.title: Fix (AI remediation)
features.description: Uses AI to automate remediation of flagged vulnerabilities and save developer time.

features.title: Package Firewall
features.description: Proactively secures development pipelines against risky packages.

features.title: Risk Manager (ASPM)
features.description: Provides unified visibility and remediation tracking of application risk across the organization.

features.title: Penetration Testing as a Service (PTaaS)
features.description: Offers penetration testing alongside Security Labs and eLearning modules for developer training.

pros: Combines SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, and PTaaS in one platform
AI-powered Fix feature aims to automate vulnerability remediation
Reports a low 1.1% false-positive rate
States it has scanned over 1.5M applications and 471T+ lines of code, with 148M+ flaws fixed
Used by enterprises including Sitecore, Unisys, BMW, and Garmin, per the site
cons: No public pricing is disclosed; must contact sales or request a demo for a quote
No free trial information is provided on the site
Specific integrations and supported languages are not enumerated on the main pages reviewed

faqs.question: How much does Veracode cost?
faqs.answer: Veracode does not publish pricing; interested organizations must contact sales or request a demo for a custom quote.

faqs.question: Does Veracode offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial availability is stated on the Veracode website.

faqs.question: What testing types does Veracode support?
faqs.answer: Veracode offers SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, penetration testing (PTaaS), and AI-powered remediation via its Fix feature.

faqs.question: Does Veracode use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Veracode's Fix feature uses AI to automate remediation of security flaws to save developer time.

faqs.question: What is Veracode's reported false-positive rate?
faqs.answer: Veracode states a 1.1% false-positive rate on its website.

use_cases.title: Find and fix flaws throughout the SDLC
use_cases.description: Teams use SAST and DAST to catch vulnerabilities during coding and at runtime.

use_cases.title: Secure open-source and container dependencies
use_cases.description: SCA and Container Security scan third-party code and container images for known vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Accelerate remediation with AI
use_cases.description: The Fix feature uses AI to automate fixing flagged vulnerabilities, reducing manual developer effort.

use_cases.title: Track application risk across the organization
use_cases.description: Risk Manager provides ASPM-style unified visibility into application security posture.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Veracode?
paragraphs: Veracode is an application security platform providing SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, and penetration testing (PTaaS), along with an AI-powered Fix feature for automating vulnerability remediation.

Veracode reports a 1.1% false-positive rate, having scanned over 1.5 million applications and 471 trillion lines of code, and having helped fix over 148 million software flaws. Its customers include enterprises such as Sitecore, Unisys, BMW, and Garmin.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Veracode is for
paragraphs: Veracode is built for enterprise security and development teams that need to find and fix vulnerabilities across custom code, open-source dependencies, and containers, with AI-assisted remediation and centralized risk visibility.

enrichment.short_description: Gitleaks is a free, open-source CLI tool that scans git repos, files, and directories for hardcoded secrets like API keys and passwords.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gitleaks: free open-source (MIT) CLI tool to detect secrets like API keys and passwords in git history, files, and CI/CD pipelines.

features.title: Regex-based secret detection
features.description: Detects secrets such as passwords, API keys, and tokens using configurable regex rules.

features.title: Git history scanning
features.description: Scans full git history using `git log -p` to find secrets committed in the past.

features.title: Directory and stdin scanning
features.description: Can scan directories/files directly or process streamed data via stdin.

features.title: Pre-commit hook integration
features.description: Runs as a pre-commit hook to catch secrets before they are committed.

features.title: GitHub Actions support
features.description: Integrates with CI pipelines via the companion gitleaks-action.

features.title: Multiple report formats
features.description: Outputs findings as JSON, CSV, JUnit, SARIF, or custom templates for CI/CD tooling.

features.title: Baseline comparisons
features.description: Supports baseline files so only new findings are reported on incremental scans.

features.title: Configurable allowlists
features.description: TOML-based configuration supports global and rule-specific allowlists, plus a `.gitleaksignore` file.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Widely adopted, with 28.2k stars on GitHub
Multiple scan modes (git, directory, stdin) cover different workflows
CI/CD-friendly report formats including SARIF and JUnit
Pre-commit and GitHub Actions integrations available out of the box
cons: Project is described by its maintainers as 'feature complete,' with future releases limited to security patches
CLI-only — no built-in GUI or hosted dashboard
No official commercial support or SLA offering tied to the open-source project

faqs.question: Is Gitleaks free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Gitleaks is open source under the MIT license with no paid tiers.

faqs.question: What can Gitleaks scan?
faqs.answer: It can scan git repository history, local directories/files, and data piped in via stdin.

faqs.question: Does Gitleaks integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faqs.answer: Yes, via a pre-commit hook, the gitleaks-action for GitHub Actions, and report formats like SARIF and JUnit.

faqs.question: Is Gitleaks still actively developed?
faqs.answer: The project's maintainers describe it as feature complete, with future releases limited to security patches.

faqs.question: Can detection rules be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes, rules are configured via TOML files and support global or rule-specific allowlists.

use_cases.title: Blocking secrets before they're committed
use_cases.description: Run Gitleaks as a pre-commit hook to catch API keys and passwords before code is committed.

use_cases.title: CI pipeline secret scanning
use_cases.description: Add Gitleaks to a GitHub Actions workflow to scan pull requests and generate SARIF/JUnit reports.

use_cases.title: Auditing existing repository history
use_cases.description: Run Gitleaks in git mode against `git log -p` to find secrets already committed in the past.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gitleaks?
paragraphs: Gitleaks is an open-source, MIT-licensed command-line tool that detects secrets such as passwords, API keys, and tokens in git repositories, directories, files, and stdin input.

It is distributed via Homebrew, Docker (DockerHub and ghcr.io), direct binary downloads, or built from source, and its detection rules are configured through TOML files with support for allowlists and a `.gitleaksignore` file.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gitleaks is for
paragraphs: Gitleaks is used by developers and DevOps/security teams who need a free, scriptable way to catch hardcoded secrets in git history and codebases, whether locally via pre-commit hooks or in CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions.

enrichment.short_description: TruffleHog is a secret-scanning tool from Truffle Security Co. that finds and verifies exposed credentials across git repos, Docker images, and cloud storage.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TruffleHog scans code, Docker images, and cloud storage for secrets and verifies them live with 800+ detectors. Free open-source core plus Enterprise tier.

features.title: Deep repository scanning
features.description: Scans git version history, Docker images, and comments to find exposed secrets, not just the latest commit.

features.title: Live credential verification
features.description: Programmatically verifies discovered secrets against the relevant service/API to eliminate false positives.

features.title: 800+ secret detectors
features.description: Ships with detectors for 800+ secret types out of the box.

features.title: Multi-branch scanning
features.description: Analyzes all branches in a repository, not just the primary branch.

features.title: Pre-commit and pre-receive hooks
features.description: Blocks secrets from being committed or pushed in the first place.

features.title: TruffleHog Analyze
features.description: Automatically identifies the resources and permissions tied to a found API key or secret.

features.title: Custom regex support
features.description: Supports custom regex rules in addition to built-in detectors.

features.title: Remediation tracking
features.description: Tracks the status of found keys and sends alert reminders with rotation guidance.

pros: Free, open-source core with 800+ built-in secret detectors
Verifies secrets live against real services/APIs to cut down false positives
Scans across full git history, multiple branches, and Docker images, not just the latest commit
Pre-commit and pre-receive hook support to prevent leaks before they happen
Enterprise tier adds SSO, RBAC, a management dashboard, and 20+ integrations for larger teams
cons: Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Dashboard, continuous monitoring, SSO/RBAC, and most integrations are Enterprise-only, not in the open-source version
Add-ons like TruffleHog Analyze and Forager are sold separately from the core scanner

faqs.question: Is TruffleHog free to use?
faqs.answer: The open-source core is free, offering GitHub, S3, directory, GCS, and Docker scanning with 800+ detectors.

faqs.question: What does TruffleHog scan?
faqs.answer: It scans GitHub, S3, local directories, GCS, and Docker images/history for exposed secrets.

faqs.question: Does TruffleHog verify the secrets it finds?
faqs.answer: Yes, it uses programmatic verification against the relevant service or API to confirm a secret is live, reducing false positives.

faqs.question: What does the Enterprise tier add?
faqs.answer: Enterprise adds 20+ integrations (GitHub, Confluence, JIRA, Slack, etc.), on-premises/cloud deployment choice, continuous monitoring, a management dashboard, SSO, RBAC, and priority support.

faqs.question: Who makes TruffleHog?
faqs.answer: TruffleHog is made by Truffle Security Co., founded in 2021.

use_cases.title: Pre-deployment secret scanning
use_cases.description: Scan git repositories and Docker images for exposed credentials before code ships.
use_cases.audience: Developers and security teams using the open-source scanner

use_cases.title: Preventing leaks at commit time
use_cases.description: Use pre-commit and pre-receive hooks to stop secrets from ever being committed or pushed.

use_cases.title: Enterprise-wide secret governance
use_cases.description: Continuously monitor secrets across an organization with SSO, RBAC, and a centralized dashboard.
use_cases.audience: Security and compliance teams on the Enterprise tier

features: GitHub, S3, directory, GCS, and Docker scanning
GitHub Actions, pre-commit, and pre-receive hooks

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TruffleHog?
paragraphs: TruffleHog is a secret-scanning tool made by Truffle Security Co., founded in 2021, that digs into code repositories, Docker images, and cloud storage to find exposed passwords, API keys, and other sensitive credentials.
Its open-source core includes 800+ secret detectors and live credential verification, while a separate Enterprise tier adds integrations, SSO/RBAC, continuous monitoring, and a management dashboard.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TruffleHog is for
paragraphs: Developers and security teams use the free open-source scanner to catch leaked secrets in repos, branches, and Docker images, while organizations needing centralized governance, SSO, and monitoring can upgrade to the Enterprise tier.

enrichment.short_description: Akeyless is a SaaS platform for secrets management, machine identity security, PKI, and multi-cloud key management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Akeyless offers secrets management, PAM, certificate lifecycle, and multi-cloud KMS with a free plan and custom Enterprise pricing.

features.title: Secrets Management
features.description: Centrally stores and dynamically delivers static, dynamic, and rotated secrets to applications and pipelines.

features.title: Distributed Fragments Cryptography
features.description: Patented encryption technology that fragments cryptographic keys across regions.

features.title: SecretlessAI
features.description: Keeps secrets out of AI agent context while validating agent actions at runtime via Agentic Runtime Authority.

features.title: Certificate Lifecycle Management
features.description: Issues, rotates, and manages SSH and PKI certificates, with CA integration.

features.title: Multi-cloud Key Management (KMS)
features.description: Manages and performs encryption operations using keys across multiple cloud providers.

features.title: Universal Secrets Connector
features.description: Provides centralized governance across multiple third-party vaults.

features.title: Privileged Access Management
features.description: Zero-trust, just-in-time access controls for privileged sessions.

pros: Offers a Free plan covering multiple product modules, not just a single feature.
Combines secrets management, PAM, PKI, and KMS in one platform.
Holds compliance certifications including ISO 27001, PCI-DSS, FIPS 140-3, GDPR, and DORA.
Provides SDKs and integrations for CI/CD, Kubernetes, and identity providers.
cons: Free plan quotas are restrictive (e.g., up to 5 clients, 500 static secrets, 3-day audit log retention).
Enterprise plan pricing is not published and requires contacting sales for a custom quote.
Encryption/KMS free tier is capped at 1,000 transactions per day.

faqs.question: Does Akeyless offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Akeyless has a Free plan with limited quotas across modules, such as up to 5 clients, 500 static secrets, and 3-day audit log retention.

faqs.question: What is SecretlessAI?
faqs.answer: SecretlessAI is Akeyless's approach to keeping secrets out of AI agents' context while still validating and authorizing their actions at runtime.

faqs.question: Is Akeyless Enterprise pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, the Enterprise plan offers customizable quotas and pricing that requires contacting Akeyless directly.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Akeyless hold?
faqs.answer: Akeyless states it holds ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI-DSS, AICPA, CSA STAR Level One, FIPS 140-3, and DORA compliance.

use_cases.title: Secrets management for DevOps pipelines
use_cases.description: Centrally store and dynamically deliver static, dynamic, and rotated secrets to applications and CI/CD tooling.

use_cases.title: Securing AI agent access
use_cases.description: Use SecretlessAI and Agentic Runtime Authority to keep credentials out of AI agents while validating their actions.

use_cases.title: Certificate lifecycle automation
use_cases.description: Automatically issue, rotate, and manage SSH and PKI certificates with CA integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Akeyless?
paragraphs: Akeyless is a SaaS platform for secrets management, machine identity security, certificate lifecycle management, and multi-cloud key management (KMS).
It uses patented Distributed Fragments Cryptography and offers a Free plan alongside a customizable Enterprise plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Akeyless is for
paragraphs: Akeyless is built for DevOps, security, and platform engineering teams that need to manage secrets, certificates, encryption keys, and privileged access across multi-cloud environments, including organizations securing AI agent workflows.

enrichment.short_description: HashiCorp Vault secures, stores, and tightly controls access to tokens, passwords, certificates, and encryption keys.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HashiCorp Vault manages secrets, certificates, and encryption keys with a free open-source Community edition and paid Enterprise/HCP options.

features.title: Key/Value Secrets Engine
features.description: Stores and versions static secrets accessible via UI, CLI, or HTTP API.

features.title: Database Secrets Engine
features.description: Dynamically generates short-lived database credentials on demand.

features.title: Transit Secrets Engine
features.description: Provides encryption as a service for data in transit without storing the data itself.

features.title: Transform Secrets Engine
features.description: An Enterprise-only engine that supports tokenization of sensitive data.

features.title: Identity-based access control
features.description: Policy-driven authentication and authorization for humans, machines, and AI agents.

features.title: Certificate management
features.description: Generates, rotates, and revokes certificates on demand.

pros: Vault Community edition is free and open source.
Covers multiple secrets engines (Key/Value, Database, Transit) for varied use cases.
HCP Vault Dedicated offers a cloud-hosted, managed option with a free trial.
Backed by official certification programs (Vault Associate, Vault Operations Professional).
cons: Enterprise-only features, such as the Transform Secrets Engine, require a paid license.
Public pricing for Vault Enterprise and HCP Vault is not listed on the product or pricing pages.
Company founding year and headquarters are not published on the pages reviewed.

faqs.question: Is HashiCorp Vault free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vault Community edition is free and open source; Vault Enterprise requires a valid paid license.

faqs.question: What does Vault do?
faqs.answer: Vault secures, stores, and tightly controls access to tokens, passwords, certificates, and encryption keys via a UI, CLI, or HTTP API.

faqs.question: Does HCP Vault offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, HCP Vault Dedicated is referenced as offering a free trial option.

faqs.question: What is the Transit Secrets Engine?
faqs.answer: It provides encryption as a service, letting Vault encrypt and decrypt data in transit without Vault storing the underlying data.

use_cases.title: Centralized secrets management
use_cases.description: Store and programmatically access tokens, passwords, and API keys from a single platform.

use_cases.title: Dynamic database credentials
use_cases.description: Generate short-lived, on-demand database credentials using the Database Secrets Engine.

use_cases.title: Encryption as a service
use_cases.description: Use the Transit Secrets Engine to encrypt and decrypt application data without directly managing keys.

features: Key/Value, Database, and Transit secrets engines

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HashiCorp Vault?
paragraphs: HashiCorp Vault is a secrets management platform that secures, stores, and tightly controls access to tokens, passwords, certificates, and encryption keys via a UI, CLI, or HTTP API.
It is available as a free, open-source Community edition, with a paid Enterprise edition and HCP Vault cloud-hosted options for additional features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HashiCorp Vault is for
paragraphs: Vault is built for DevOps, security, and platform teams that need identity-based secrets management, dynamic database credentials, and encryption as a service across humans, machines, and AI agents.

enrichment.short_description: A 1Password developer feature for automating secrets access in apps and cloud infrastructure via Service Accounts or self-hosted Connect servers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: 1Password Secrets Automation: automate secrets access for apps and infrastructure using Service Accounts or self-hosted Connect servers.

features.title: Service Accounts
features.description: An authentication method for the 1Password CLI that isn't tied to an individual user, letting teams automate secrets management without deploying additional infrastructure.

features.title: Connect servers
features.description: A self-hosted alternative that caches data in your own infrastructure, giving more control, scalability, and reduced dependency on the 1Password API, plus REST API access not available with Service Accounts.

features.title: 1Password CLI integration
features.description: Both Service Accounts and Connect servers work through the 1Password CLI for scripting and automation.

features.title: CI/CD pipeline integrations
features.description: Pre-built integrations sync secrets into pipelines such as GitHub Actions, CircleCI, and Jenkins, with the ability to build custom integrations.

features.title: Kubernetes integration
features.description: Documented support for syncing secrets into Kubernetes environments.

features.title: Secret references
features.description: Developers can reference secrets stored in 1Password instead of hardcoding credentials in application code.

features.title: IDE extensions
features.description: Extensions help developers work with APIs and databases while detecting exposed secrets before they're committed to a repository.

pros: Included with a 1Password subscription rather than sold as a separate line item
Service Accounts require no additional infrastructure to deploy
Connect servers give teams control over caching and reduce reliance on 1Password's API availability
Pre-built CI/CD integrations for common tools like GitHub Actions, CircleCI, and Jenkins
CLI-based workflow supports automating tasks such as credential rotation
cons: Service Accounts use a rate-limited access model
Connect servers must be self-hosted by the customer, adding operational overhead
REST API access is only available through Connect, not Service Accounts
No dedicated public pricing page separate from general 1Password subscription plans

faqs.question: What is 1Password Secrets Automation?
faqs.answer: It is a set of tools for securely accessing 1Password items and vaults from a company's applications and cloud infrastructure, using either Service Accounts or Connect servers.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Service Accounts and Connect servers?
faqs.answer: Service Accounts are a low-overhead, rate-limited authentication method for the 1Password CLI with no infrastructure to deploy, while Connect servers are self-hosted, cache data locally, and expose a REST API.

faqs.question: Does Secrets Automation cost extra?
faqs.answer: According to 1Password's documentation, it is included with a 1Password subscription rather than billed separately.

faqs.question: Which CI/CD tools integrate with Secrets Automation?
faqs.answer: 1Password documents pre-built integrations for GitHub Actions, CircleCI, and Jenkins, along with support for building custom integrations.

use_cases.title: CI/CD secret syncing
use_cases.description: Automatically sync secrets into CI/CD pipelines instead of storing them as plaintext pipeline variables.

use_cases.title: Infrastructure secret orchestration
use_cases.description: Load secrets into server applications and cloud infrastructure using Connect servers or Service Accounts.

use_cases.title: Preventing hardcoded credentials
use_cases.description: Replace hardcoded credentials in source code with secret references resolved via the CLI or SDKs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is 1Password Secrets Automation?
paragraphs: 1Password Secrets Automation is a developer-focused capability within 1Password for securely accessing items and vaults from a company's applications and cloud infrastructure.

It offers two approaches: Service Accounts, a low-overhead authentication method for the 1Password CLI that requires no additional infrastructure, and Connect servers, a self-hosted option that caches data locally and exposes a REST API for more control and scalability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who 1Password Secrets Automation is for
paragraphs: It is aimed at developers and infrastructure teams who need to automate secrets access in CI/CD pipelines, Kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure without hardcoding credentials, and who are already using 1Password for credential management.

enrichment.short_description: Open source credential and password manager for teams, offered as a free self-hosted Community Edition or paid Pro/Enterprise plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Passbolt: open source password and credential manager for teams, with a free Community Edition and paid Pro/Enterprise plans.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: Uses public-private key architecture to encrypt stored credentials end-to-end.

features.title: Granular credential sharing
features.description: Fine-grained permissions for sharing passwords and credentials, with folders and user/group management.

features.title: Just-In-Time access
features.description: Supports Just-In-Time (JIT) access and principle-of-least-privilege enforcement for credential access.

features.title: Activity monitoring and audit trails
features.description: Real-time activity monitoring and logging (activity logging available on Pro/Enterprise editions).

features.title: Multifactor authentication
features.description: MFA enabled by default, along with brute-force and phishing protection.

features.title: Browser extensions and apps
features.description: Available as browser extensions, desktop applications, native mobile apps, and a web interface.

features.title: API, CLI, and SDK access
features.description: API-centric architecture with CLI and SDK support for automated secret retrieval, rotation, and CI/CD integration.

features.title: LDAP and SSO (Pro/Enterprise)
features.description: Pro and Enterprise editions add LDAP provisioning and SSO via Microsoft, Google, or OpenID.

pros: 100% open source under the AGPLv3 license with a free, unlimited-user Community Edition
Self-hosted deployment option, including support for air-gapped environments
SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant
Used by over 50,000 organizations, including government agencies and enterprises
Cloud hosting option available, based in Europe
cons: Pro Edition requires a minimum of 10 users and is limited to a single self-hosted instance
Enterprise pricing is custom and requires contacting sales
SSO and LDAP provisioning are not included in the free Community Edition
Losing an active subscription removes access to updates and support

faqs.question: Is Passbolt free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free with unlimited users under the AGPLv3 open source license, though it has community-level support only.

faqs.question: What does the Pro Edition cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro Edition costs €4.50 per user/month when billed annually, with a minimum of 10 users and volume discounts after 100 users.

faqs.question: Can Passbolt be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Community and Pro editions support self-hosted, on-premises deployment, including air-gapped environments; a separate cloud-hosted option is also available.

faqs.question: Does Passbolt offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available for the Pro Edition according to Passbolt's pricing page.

faqs.question: Is Passbolt compliant with security standards?
faqs.answer: Passbolt states it is SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant, and a FIDO Alliance member, with regular third-party security audits.

use_cases.title: Team password management
use_cases.description: Centralized, encrypted storage and sharing of team passwords with granular permissions and group management.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted/air-gapped credential storage
use_cases.description: Organizations with strict data residency or compliance needs can self-host Passbolt, including in air-gapped environments.
use_cases.audience: Government agencies, defense organizations, and regulated enterprises

use_cases.title: Automated secret retrieval in CI/CD
use_cases.description: Use the API, CLI, and SDKs to retrieve and rotate secrets programmatically within CI/CD pipelines.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Passbolt?
paragraphs: Passbolt is an open source credential platform built for security-conscious IT teams that is also simple enough for company-wide adoption, using end-to-end encryption based on public-private key architecture.

It is offered as a free, unlimited-user Community Edition, alongside paid Pro and Enterprise editions with additional features like SSO, LDAP provisioning, and dedicated support. It can be self-hosted, including in air-gapped environments, or used via a Europe-based cloud-hosted option.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Passbolt is for
paragraphs: Passbolt is used by IT teams, government agencies, defense organizations, universities, and enterprises that need granular, auditable credential sharing, with over 50,000 organizations reported as users.
